functions.name: researchgraph.graphs.ai_scientist.ai_scientist_node.generate_ideas.on_backoff
documentation: # seed the archive on the first run with pre-existing ideas
Completed ideas have an additional "Score" field which indicates the assessment by an expert ML reviewer.
This is on a standard 1-10 ML conference scale.
Scores of 0 indicate the idea failed either during experimentation, writeup or reviewing.
assert json_output is not None, "Failed to extract JSON from LLM output"
), "Failed to extract JSON from LLM output"

methods.name: researchgraph.graphs.ai_scientist.templates.nanoGPT_lite.experiment.GPT.get_num_params
documentation:
Return the number of parameters in the model.
For non-embedding count (default), the position embeddings get subtracted.
The token embeddings would too, except due to the parameter sharing these
params are actually used as weights in the final layer, so we include them.

methods.name: researchgraph.graphs.ai_scientist.templates.nanoGPT_lite.experiment.GPT.generate
documentation:
Take a conditioning sequence of indices idx (LongTensor of shape (b,t)) and complete
the sequence max_new_tokens times, feeding the predictions back into the model each time.
Most likely you'll want to make sure to be in model.eval() mode of operation for this.

classes.name: researchgraph.graphs.ai_scientist.templates.2d_diffusion.ema_pytorch.EMA
documentation:
Implements exponential moving average shadowing for your model.

Utilizes an inverse decay schedule to manage longer term training runs.
By adjusting the power, you can control how fast EMA will ramp up to your specified beta.

@crowsonkb's notes on EMA Warmup:

If gamma=1 and power=1, implements a simple average. gamma=1, power=2/3 are
good values for models you plan to train for a million or more steps (reaches decay
factor 0.999 at 31.6K steps, 0.9999 at 1M steps), gamma=1, power=3/4 for models
you plan to train for less (reaches decay factor 0.999 at 10K steps, 0.9999 at
215.4k steps).

Args:
    inv_gamma (float): Inverse multiplicative factor of EMA warmup. Default: 1.
    power (float): Exponential factor of EMA warmup. Default: 2/3.
    min_value (float): The minimum EMA decay rate. Default: 0.
